preview_text:
The model favors Netherlands decisively, assigning them a 68% win probability against Sweden's 8%. The gap reflects a substantial edge to the Dutch side, with the underdog facing a steep climb to claim three points.

The win-draw-loss distribution shows moderate separation between the favorite and second-most-likely outcome. A draw sits at 24%, suggesting the match carries genuine competitive uncertainty despite Netherlands' clear advantage. Sweden's 8% win probability indicates the model views a Swedish victory as unlikely but not negligible; the match is not expected to be a rout, but a comfortable Dutch progression appears the central scenario.

The model and market diverge meaningfully on both tails. The model assigns Netherlands 10 percentage points higher than sportsbook consensus, while crediting Sweden 10 points lower. Both Polymarket and the 42-book consensus cluster closely on the draw near 23–24%. The dashboard makes no claim about which source better reflects true match probability; the divergence suggests genuine disagreement on Netherlands' win probability specifically.
